.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:92:{"acceptance":"File library/personas/test-engineer.md exists with valid YAML frontmatter. Body covers the core stance: favor real e2e tests (no mocks at e2e layer); stubs over mocks at unit layer; contract tests between stub and real backend; zero mocks in integration tests; behavior over implementation; deterministic factories; behavior-naming; testing pyramid as shape not ratios; performance measurements require baseline + explicit boundary + reproducible harness. grep -E \"cli/internal/|docs/helix/|orchTestGitOps|fakeExecuteBeadGit|gateTestGitOps|mockExecutor|script\\.go|integration_helper_test\" library/personas/test-engineer.md returns no matches. No hard numeric coverage threshold presented as a universal rule. No \"TDD is the only valid approach\" framing. No retry-loop-around-flaky-test as acceptable pattern. No performance claim pattern without baseline + boundary. Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).\n\nRename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.\n\n**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**\n- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); Rewrite with real stances.\n\nCore stances:\n- Simplicity beats sophistication — the right architecture is the simplest one that works for the real load, not the one that scales to 10x imagined load\n- Prefer monolith first — microservices have operational cost that only pays back at real scale\n- Event-driven only when async decoupling earns it — not by default\n- Data model beats clever caching — design the schema right, the cache falls out naturally\n- Write ADRs for decisions that will bite future maintainers; The existing file has a generic tutorial tone; rewrite with real opinions.\n\nCore stances:\n- YAGNI — implement only what is specified; no speculative features or \"while we are here\" additions\n- KISS — simplest solution that meets requirements; avoid clever code and premature optimization\n- DOWITYTD (Do Only What I Told You To Do) — stop when the task is complete\n- Ship tests with code — implementations without a test command referenced in AC are half-done (but: test-first vs test-alongside vs test-after is a workflow choice, not mandated by this persona — see the test-engineer persona for the testing stance; this persona requires tests exist and pass, not a specific methodology)\n- Do not design for hypothetical future requirements. Three similar lines is better than a premature abstraction.\n- **Performance claims require baselines.** Do not add code \"for performance\" without a baseline measurement and a clear boundary describing what is being measured. \"This is faster\" without a number is not a justification. (Cross-reference test-engineer for the measurement discipline.)\n- **Favor real e2e validation at the integration boundary.** When in doubt between a layer of abstraction/mocking and writing a real integration test, write the real integration test. The cost of mocks compounds; the cost of a real e2e test is paid once.\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nAnti-patterns to call out:\n- Speculative abstraction (base classes, interfaces, config knobs for \"someday\")\n- Refactoring unrelated code during a bug fix (\"while we are here...\")\n- Adding error handling for scenarios that cannot happen (trust internal invariants; validate at system boundaries)\n- Leaving TODO comments for problems that should block the current change\n- Comments that restate what the code does (well-named identifiers already do that)\n- Adding a mock or stub when a real integration test would be simpler and catch more bugs\n- Making performance claims without baselines or boundaries\n\nSources:\n- YAGNI principle (c2.com wiki, various Martin Fowler articles)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library entries for coding agents, if any fit\n- Relevant DDx system-prompt content from library/prompts/claude/system-prompts/focused.md (already documents YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD)\n- Brendan Gregg on performance measurement discipline (baseline + boundary)\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: implementer\nroles: [implementer, software-engineer]\ndescription: Ships the specified scope, nothing more — YAGNI/KISS/DOWITYTD with tests included, real e2e favored over mocks, performance claims only with baselines\ntags: [implementation, shipping, minimalism]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/implementer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: Deleting pragmatic-implementer.md (deprecation bead); other persona rewrites (separate beads)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:41:46.437282625Z","id":"ddx-2da7da89","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Rewrite implementer persona with opinionated YAGNI/KISS stance","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:45:00.750092403Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:373:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer opening the document graph, I want a structured integrity panel that groups real document problems by kind and points me at the files to fix, so I can triage a noisy graph quickly and confirm when it is healthy.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` (or a sibling `Issues` field) is a typed slice with `Kind`, `Path`, `ID`, `Message`, and optional `RelatedPath`. Kinds include at minimum: `duplicate_id`, `parse_error`, `missing_dep`, `id_path_missing`, `id_path_mismatch`, `required_root_missing`, `cascade_unknown`.\n2. GraphQL schema exposes `DocGraph.issues: [GraphIssue!]!` with the same fields. The existing string `warnings` either stays (rendered from issues) or is deprecated with a schema comment.\n3. Unit tests in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph_test.go` cover each issue kind: fixtures that produce exactly one instance of that kind and assert the typed output.\n4. `/graph` page renders an **Integrity** panel when `issues.length \u003e 0`:\n   - grouped by `kind` with a count per group;\n   - each row shows path, id, and message;\n   - path links to the document viewer (reuses existing documents route);\n   - total-issues badge appears next to the page title.\n5. With zero issues, neither the panel nor the badge renders, and the graph view is visually identical to a healthy graph today.\n6. Duplicate-ID rows show both paths and a \"copy suggested unique ID\" button; the suggestion is deterministic (same input → same output) and a unit test asserts the suggestion function's stability.\n7. `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`) CLI command prints the same issue list grouped by kind, exits 0 if empty and 1 if any issues present. Integration test asserts exit codes and grouped output.\n8. Playwright e2e:\n   - navigates to `/graph` on a fixture repo with one seeded duplicate-id pair and one missing-dep target;\n   - asserts the Integrity panel shows groups \"Duplicate ID (1)\" and \"Missing dep target (1)\";\n   - expands the Duplicate ID group and asserts both paths are visible;\n   - clicks the first path and asserts navigation to the documents route for that file;\n   - on a clean fixture repo, asserts neither the panel nor the badge is present.\n\n**Dependency:** best sequenced after `ddx-12cae4dd` lands so the fixture repos are not polluted by `.claude/worktrees` noise, but implementation does not block on it — the two can proceed in parallel and this bead's tests use synthetic fixtures.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-23T04:15:59Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3704155","closing_commit_sha":"8b291ee475105211ea3b3369e7bf0e88c82166a8","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:09:46.473146653Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe graph view at `/nodes/.../projects/.../graph` renders a long flat list of amber-styled warnings, currently dominated by `duplicate document id \"AC-AGENT-001\" in \"/Users/erik/Projects/ddx/.claude/worktrees/agent-a2818c5c/docs/resources/agent-harness-ac.md\"` and similar entries. That fix will eliminate a large class of false positives but will not make the warning surface usable.\n2. **Brittle degradation** — when documents have real problems (duplicate IDs in the canonical tree, broken frontmatter, missing `depends_on` targets, `id_to_path` mismatches), the system doesn't just warn — downstream consumers (documents list, staleness, UI routing) misbehave. And there is no workflow for a user to triage, diff, or fix the offending files.\n\n## Current state of warnings\n\n- `cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/graph/+page.svelte:22-30` dumps `graph.warnings: string[]` into a flat amber block. No grouping, no categorization, no links to the offending file, no way to dismiss or act.\n- Warning strings are generated in `cli/internal/docgraph/docgraph.go` (duplicate id, parse failure, required-root-missing, id_to_path mismatch/missing, cascade unknowns) as unstructured `fmt.Sprintf` output.\n- No stale-document-pruning: if a `.md` is deleted on disk, any id_to_path entry pointing at it yields a warning instead of being auto-cleaned.\n\n## Scope of this bead (deliberately not overlapping ddx-12cae4dd)\n\nAssume `.claude/worktrees` noise is gone. This bead delivers the **integrity surface** that remains valuable: a way to see, categorize, and act on real document problems.\n\n### Deliverables\n\n1. **Structured warnings** — extend `docgraph.Graph.Warnings` from `[]string` to `[]GraphIssue{kind, path, id, message, relatedPath?}` and plumb through GraphQL. Each group shows count + expandable list; each row shows file path (clickable → document viewer), the offending ID, and a copyable message.\n3. **Summary badge** — small badge next to the page title showing total issue count, so a clean graph reads clean.\n4. **Audit GraphQL query** — `query { docGraphIssues { kind, path, id, message, relatedPath } }` usable independently of the full graph for future dashboard use. Reuses the same structured slice.\n5. **Repair affordances (minimum viable)** — for two common cases:\n   - Duplicate ID: show both file paths side-by-side and a \"copy suggested unique ID\" button (deterministic suggestion, e.g., append a short hash of the path). No auto-write.\n   - Missing dep target: show the document declaring the missing dep, and a copy-to-clipboard snippet of the frontmatter line to remove. No auto-write.\n   Auto-repair is explicitly out of scope until we trust the detection.\n6. **CLI parity** — `ddx doctor` (or `ddx docs audit`) prints the same structured issues in a terminal-friendly form, so headless users and CI can surface problems.\n\n### Out of scope\n\n- Fixing `.claude/worktrees` indexing (owned by `ddx-12cae4dd`).\n- Auto-rewrite of source files.\n- Cross-project graph integrity (today's graph is per-project).","events_attachment":"ddx-6ff597ca/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-23T04:26:29.781059658Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","feature":"FEAT-007","id":"ddx-6ff597ca","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-007","feat-008","ui","docgraph"],"notes":"**AC 7 — command path**: The command is registered as `ddx doc audit` (`cli/cmd/doc.go:59`), but the acceptance criteria specifies `ddx docs audit` (or `ddx doctor docs`). .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:523:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer looking at the personas UI, I want to understand at a glance what personas are, where they come from (library vs project), and I want to fork or create a project-local persona without leaving the UI. Library content stays read-only so I cannot accidentally mutate shared content; project content is fully editable.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Design note + policy decision recorded.** Before implementation, a one-page note in `docs/helix/02-design/` captures D1 (library vs project policy, override rules, file locations) and D2 (scoped UI additions). Implementation PR links to it.\n\n2. **Project-local persona directory.** `.ddx/personas/*.md` is a recognized location. `ddx persona --list` shows both library and project personas with a `source` tag. Override rule: a project persona with the same name as a library persona wins for that project's bindings; unit test covers this precedence.\n\n3. **GraphQL additions.** New mutations: `personaCreate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaUpdate(name, body, projectId) → Persona`, `personaDelete(name, projectId) → {ok}`. All gated on `source == \"project\"`; attempting any of them on a library persona returns a typed error. Each mutation persists to `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` under the given project.\n\n4. **CLI parity.** `ddx persona new`, `edit`, `fork`, `delete` exist and share the same underlying operations as the GraphQL mutations (no duplicated logic). Integration test asserts that a `ddx persona new` followed by a GraphQL `personas` query returns the new persona.\n\n5. **UI — explainer copy.** The personas page shows a one-sentence description of what personas are and a second sentence distinguishing library vs project sources. Playwright asserts both sentences are present.\n\n6. **UI — source badge.** Each persona row shows a `library` or `project` badge. Playwright seeds one of each and asserts correct badges.\n\n7. **UI — New / Edit / Delete for project personas.** A \"New persona\" button opens a form with name + body fields. Project-source rows have Edit and Delete affordances. Library-source rows have neither. Playwright creates a persona, edits it, verifies the edit persisted, then deletes it.\n\n8. **UI — Fork library to project.** Library rows have a \"Fork to project\" action. Clicking it prompts for a name (default: library name with `-local` suffix on collision), creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md`, and navigates to the editor. Playwright asserts the forked file exists and opens in the editor.\n\n9. **No regressions.** Existing list/show/bind flows still work. Playwright smoke of the pre-existing binding UX passes.\n\n10. **Empty project-local state.** When a project has zero project-local personas, the UI shows a subtle hint: \"No project personas yet. Fork a library persona or create a new one.\" Playwright asserts.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"69c60d902a41f3a24af908881f4ef14ba1ff3832","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:30:40.66472156Z","description":"## Observed\n\n`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas` is a well-crafted browser + bind UX, but it is read-only: no add, no edit, no delete, no explanation of what the reader is looking at or why they cannot change it. A user looking at it can reasonably ask \"what is this for?\" and \"why can't I change anything?\" and the UI answers neither.\n\n## Ground truth\n\n- **What a persona is.** A markdown file with frontmatter under `library/personas/*.md` defining AI personality, expertise, principles, communication style. Shipped defaults: architect, code-reviewer, implementer, specification-enforcer, test-engineer.\n- **How it's used.** Projects declare role→persona bindings in `.ddx/config.yaml` via `persona_bindings`. When an agent performs role X, the bound persona's body is injected into its system prompt.\n- **What the tool exposes today.**\n  - CLI: `ddx persona --list`, `--show \u003cname\u003e`, `--bind \u003cpersona\u003e --role \u003crole\u003e`. No create/edit/delete.\n  - GraphQL: `personaBind` mutation only (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:150`). No `personaCreate/Update/Delete` in schema.\n  - UI: list/show/bind parity with CLI.\n- **Why it evolved this way.** Personas have been treated as *library content* — authored carefully, version-controlled upstream, pulled into projects via `ddx update` and contributed back via `ddx contribute`. The tool deliberately did not become a persona editor.\n\n## The gap\n\nThe library-only model doesn't hold up for real use:\n\n1. **Project-local personas are a real need.** Teams want \"our code-reviewer, with our opinions about our codebase.\" Today they either (a) fork the persona file and push upstream (wrong — it's not generally useful), (b) never customize (bad outcomes), or (c) drop a file into some ad-hoc local path and hope it works.\n2. **The UI implies capability it doesn't have.** A list + detail view with no hint about source policy reads as \"unfinished feature\" rather than \"intentional read-only\".\n3. **Binding alone isn't enough.** Even if editing stays out of scope, users need to understand *which personas are available*, *where they come from* (library vs project-local), and *how to change that*.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo decisions (pick before implementation):\n\n### D1 — Policy: who owns persona content?\n\nRecommend: **both, with a clear boundary.**\n- Library personas: `library/personas/*.md` — read-only in UI, contribute upstream via `ddx contribute`.\n- Project-local personas: new directory `.ddx/personas/*.md` — fully editable in UI, live with the project, not in the shared library. Override semantics: a project-local persona with the same name as a library persona takes precedence for that project's bindings.\n\n### D2 — Scope of the UI\n\nMinimum viable, given D1:\n- **Explainer copy** on the personas page header: \"Personas are AI personality templates. Library personas are shared; project personas live with this project. Bind a persona to a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`.\"\n- **Source badge** on each persona row: `library` or `project`.\n- **Project-local editor.** For project personas:\n  - `New persona` button → form (name, roles/tags, body markdown). Creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` with frontmatter.\n  - `Edit` on project rows.\n  - `Delete` on project rows with confirm.\n- **Library rows stay read-only.** Action on a library row: `Fork to project` — copies the library file to `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` (with a default rename suggestion on collision) and opens the editor. No in-place edit, no delete.\n- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.\n\n## What this bead does NOT decide\n\n- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.\n- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.\n- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.\n- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).\n\n## CLI parity\n\nWhatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too. Add:\n- `ddx persona new \u003cname\u003e` — creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` scaffold.\n- `ddx persona edit \u003cname\u003e` — opens `$EDITOR` on the project-local persona (errors on library).\n- `ddx persona fork \u003clibrary-name\u003e [--as \u003cnew-name\u003e]` — copies library → project.\n- `ddx persona delete \u003cname\u003e` — project-local only.\n\nUI and CLI call the same underlying `cli/internal/persona` operations; no duplicate logic.","events_attachment":"ddx-9e0eff22/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-9e0eff22","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","personas","ui","design"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nThe entire diff is a single file `.ddx/executions/20260423T023822-a2d45fb0/result.json` — an execution log, not implementation code.\nZero of the required artifacts are present: no `docs/helix/02-design/` design note, no changes to `cli/internal/persona/`, no GraphQL schema or resolver additions, no SvelteKit UI changes, no Playwright tests, no CLI command additions.\nThe `result.json` claims `\"outcome\": \"task_succeeded\"` but the merged commit (`5e13d45c`) needs to be inspected — the diff provided for review shows only the evidence file, not the implementation. If the implementation landed in a prior commit that was merged, the review diff is incomplete and needs to include all commits from the bead branch.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing `docs/helix/02-design/` design note capturing D1/D2 policy and UI scope.\nMissing project-local persona support in `cli/internal/persona`, including `.ddx/personas/*.md` discovery, source tagging, override precedence, and .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:597:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T03:35:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1106936","closing_commit_sha":"8b60eb49955aa807267a5d8aa80fab5e65c8b1b6","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.589044661Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b44ca394","depends_on_id":"ddx-cf340665","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:30:57Z"}],"description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.","events_attachment":"ddx-b44ca394/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-14T03:39:25.89063588Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"agent execution failed","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-13T21:32:10Z","execution-eligible":true,"id":"ddx-b44ca394","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:bead","area:cli"],"notes":"Blocker ddx-cf340665 is closed. Ready: add type=epic filter to ReadyExecution() in cli/internal/bead/store.go + test.","owner":"ddx","parent":"ddx-cf340665","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-reconcile-1da6495","spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Prevent execute-loop from launching open epics by default","updated_at":"2026-04-14T04:24:48.965569064Z"}

Use when the user says \"do work\", \"drain the queue\", \"run the next bead\", \"execute a bead\", \"review this\", \"check against spec\", \"what's on the queue\", \"what's ready\", \"create a bead\", \"file this as work\", \"run an agent\", \"dispatch\", \"use a persona\", \"how am I doing\", \"ddx doctor\", or mentions any ddx CLI command.\n---\n\nNo argument-hint, when_to_use, context: fork, allowed-tools, disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, paths, model, effort, agent, or hooks — those are Claude-Code extensions that break on Codex.\n\nBody structure (under 500 lines):\n1. Go resolver test covers: successful repair, stale file hash rejection, path traversal rejection.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-05-01T04:16:53Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3396374","closing_commit_sha":"23d9ff9dcb56ca7f5f98e937e9daa6a775dc1f38","created_at":"2026-04-30T15:11:04.980693187Z","description":"Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.\n\nBackend mutation:\n- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult\n- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP\n- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }\n- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.\n- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.\n\nAuto-repairable strategies:\n- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter\n- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)\n- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map\n\nUI changes to IntegrityPanel:\n- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)\n- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change\n- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)\n- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast\n- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback\n\nFailure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).","events_attachment":"ddx-ce9905b7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-05-01T04:37:23.387013204Z","id":"ddx-ce9905b7","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:graphql","area:server","area:ui","area:frontend","feat-008","graph"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-2f7ecb92","spec-id":"FEAT-008","status":"closed","title":"graph integrity repair: one-click fix for missing deps and duplicate IDs","updated_at":"2026-05-01T04:37:48.828735723Z"}

Cover every intent-router entry in SKILL.md plus edge phrasings. Example rows:\n     {\"phrase\":\"what is on the queue\", \"expected_reference\":\"reference/status.md\", \"expected_cli\":\"ddx bead ready\"}\n     {\"phrase\":\"drain the queue\", \"expected_reference\":\"reference/work.md\", \"expected_cli\":\"ddx work\"}\n     {\"phrase\":\"run the next bead\", \"expected_reference\":\"reference/work.md\", \"expected_cli\":\"ddx work\"}\n     {\"phrase\":\"got any work for me\", \"expected_reference\":\"reference/work.md\", \"expected_cli\":\"ddx bead ready\"}\n     {\"phrase\":\"review this\", \"expected_reference\":\"reference/review.md\", \"expected_cli\":\"ddx bead review\"}\n     ... 10+ more covering beads, agents, status, doctor.\n2. scripts/eval-skill.sh — driver that for each row:\n     a. Runs 'ddx agent run --harness \u003ch\u003e --text \u003cphrase\u003e' against --harness claude and --harness codex.\n     b. Parses the harness output and checks whether it mentions the expected_reference filename or invokes the expected_cli command (regex or grep-based check is fine for v1; tighten later).\n     c. Reports pass/fail per row per harness. Exits non-zero on any fail.\n   Also supports a --validate flag that does agentskills.io spec conformance on skills/ddx/SKILL.md (no Claude-only frontmatter fields, body \u003c 500 lines, all referenced reference/*.md files exist, name and description present).\n3. Makefile target 'eval-skill' (in cli/Makefile or root Makefile) that runs scripts/eval-skill.sh. Add 'make eval-skill' to CI on PRs that touch skills/ddx/.\n4. CI config (.github/workflows/*.yml or equivalent) — add a step that runs make eval-skill when skills/ddx/** changes.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/evals/routing.jsonl (new)\n- scripts/eval-skill.sh (new)\n- Makefile (new or edited target)\n- .github/workflows/ci.yml or equivalent (add step)\n\nOut-of-scope:\n- The agent eval framework itself — v1 is a simple bash driver; a more sophisticated harness can come later.\n- SKILL.md content (separate bead); eval suite assumes SKILL.md exists.\n\nThe eval suite replaces the older manual 'ask Claude and Codex a few questions in a scratch dir' smoke test with CI-enforceable checks.","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:31:39.957871993Z","id":"ddx-fa04542e","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:testing","area:skills","area:ci"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Ship ddx skill eval suite (routing.jsonl + eval-skill.sh + CI)","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:32:47.969081467Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1117:{"acceptance":"FEAT-018 and SD-018 document the nested skill.* SKILL.md schema as the stable contract for bundled DDx skills, with no migration required for the shipped skills.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-09T22:11:36Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1948399","closing_commit_sha":"dacb9e0354d2cba11793f13963e33db40fbce432","created_at":"2026-04-09T21:52:20.478078234Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-018-plugin-api-stability.md:116\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: SD-018 defines stable SKILL.md frontmatter as top-level `name` and `description`, and its validation model requires those keys. Existing bundled DDx skills still use the legacy nested `skill.name` / `skill.description` schema documented in SD-011 and present in cli/internal/skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md. If install/doctor validation is implemented from SD-018 as written, built-in DDx skills will fail validation or require an undocumented breaking migration, which violates FEAT-018's backward-compatibility goal.\nSuggested fix: Update SD-018 to either adopt the existing nested `skill.*` schema as the stable contract or explicitly define a backward-compatible migration/validation rule that accepts both legacy and stable forms, with tests covering bundled DDx skills.","id":"hx-dc1ec5f0","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-018-plugin-api-stability.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: reconcile SD-018 skill schema with existing DDx skills","updated_at":"2026-04-09T22:45:20.837170947Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:1154:{"acceptance":"Given a plugin package has a valid package.yaml but a declared skill directory missing SKILL.md or required frontmatter, when `ddx install --local` or registry install runs, then the command exits non-zero, writes no installed.yaml entry, and surfaces the same structural validation error that doctor would report","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:33:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"234160","closing_commit_sha":"516fc8c8a7d25dbc47e3f8ba694bb2646b2c8d67","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:20:55.587100214Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/install.go:212\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: The new install paths only validate `package.yaml`, then proceed to symlink/copy skills without reusing the structural checks that doctor performs. `installLocal` calls `LoadPackageManifest` and then links skills, while `InstallPackage` does the same for tarball installs. As a result, malformed plugins still install successfully. Repro: a local plugin with a valid package.yaml and a declared skill directory containing `bad-skill/` but no `SKILL.md` installs successfully instead of failing fast.\nSuggested fix: Share one structural validator between install and doctor so install rejects missing `SKILL.md`, invalid frontmatter, and related contract violations before writing install state.","id":"hx-f7e65bcc","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: fail install when plugin skill metadata is invalid","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:33:45.197279785Z"}

Same-directory requirement,\n   case-sensitivity, glob discovery (`**/*.ddx.yaml`), and rationale\n   for append-vs-replace are all documented.\n\n2. Conflict rule (frontmatter + sidecar)\n   Section \"Conflict Rule\" sets the strict policy: markdown uses\n   embedded frontmatter exclusively, non-markdown uses sidecars\n   exclusively. Per epic ddx-a9d130d0 + ADR-023 (filed as ddx-XXXX): a sub-skill is needed at .agents/skills/ddx/bead-lifecycle/ (and 4 other shipped paths) carrying the rubric, examples, and judge prompts for lint + triage + refine modes. ddx try will compose this sub-skill via the runner library.

ROOT CAUSE
.agents/skills/ddx/bead-breakdown/SKILL.md (existing) is the precedent for nested workflow sub-skills under the parent ddx skill. No equivalent exists for bead authoring quality. The bead-authoring-template.md at docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md is the canonical template (per codex review) but only has 1 strong example + 1 weak example (line 98 of template).

PROPOSED FIX
1. Create sub-skill directory bead-lifecycle/ under each of 5 shipped paths (codex amendment): skills/ddx/, cli/internal/skills/ddx/, .agents/skills/ddx/, .claude/skills/ddx/, .ddx/skills/ddx/. SKILL.md + supporting files identical across all 5 (or symlinked if the build process supports it; check current bead-breakdown/ packaging).

2. SKILL.md frontmatter: name=bead-lifecycle, description="Score, classify, and refine ddx beads. Used by ddx try hooks pre-dispatch (lint mode) and post-attempt (triage mode); operator-invocable for refine mode."

3. SKILL.md body sections:
   - LINT MODE: input=bead JSON; output=JSON {score 0-8, rationale, suggested_fixes, waivers_applied}. References the 8-criterion rubric from bead-authoring-template.md.
   - TRIAGE MODE: input=bead + outcome event + session log excerpt; output=JSON {classification ∈ {already_satisfied, ambiguous, investigated_no_path, decomposed, operator_input_needed, routing, quota, transport, tests_red, merge_conflict, review_block, timeout}, recommended_action ∈ {refine_bead_and_retry, retry_with_more_context, file_children_and_supersede, escalate_to_operator, close_as_already_done, wait_and_retry, give_up}, rationale, suggested_amendments, suggested_followup_beads}.
   - REFINE MODE: input=bead + (optional) prior triage output; output=YAML diff suggesting amendments to bead description + AC.
   - WAIVER TABLE: per-bead-type criterion skips (doc-only skips named-tests + test-name; epic skips concrete-implementation; deletion skips wired-in-assertion). Codex: rubric-first, per-bead label override second.
   - 5-10 CURATED EXAMPLES under bead-lifecycle/examples/: code bug, feature, doc-only, epic, deletion/rename, no-op investigation, upstream/external work. Operator (epic owner ddx-a9d130d0) curates from existing closed beads; LLM does not pick.

4. Mode selection: invocation prompt MUST include "MODE: lint|triage|refine" as first line; SKILL.md instructs the LLM to read mode and apply only that mode's contract.

NON-SCOPE
- LLM judgment baked into Go (this bead is data + markdown only; no code)
- ddx try hook integration (separate child bead)
- Tooling to validate skill output JSON (deferred; LLMs are usually well-behaved with structured-output prompts)
- Cross-project skill packaging (DDx-only first per FEAT-015)

Sources section cites at least two external works.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1512290","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:10:38.363323602Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-13ff86c6","depends_on_id":"ddx-5241bb19","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-17T22:12:23Z"}],"description":"The existing library/personas/test-engineer-tdd.md is the strongest shipped persona content-wise, but it references DDx-internal paths that only exist in the DDx repo (docs/helix/01-frame/concerns.md, cli/internal/agent/script.go, named DDx legacy offenders like orchTestGitOps/fakeExecuteBeadGit/gateTestGitOps/mockExecutor).\n\nRename to library/personas/test-engineer.md and generalize.\n\n**CORE STANCES (load-bearing — preserve these):**\n- Favor real e2e tests. At the e2e layer, do not mock anything — real infrastructure, real processes, real network where applicable. E2e tests that mock the database or the HTTP layer are unit tests lying about their scope.\n- Stubs over mocks at the unit layer. Extract interfaces at domain boundaries; write in-memory stubs that return canned values set up by the test. Stubs do not record call sequences.\n- Contract tests between in-memory stubs and real backends — same suite runs against both, proving they are interchangeable. This is the discipline that keeps stubs honest.\n- Zero mocks in integration tests. Use real git (temp dir with scrubbed env), real filesystem (t.TempDir), real database (temp file / temp container), real HTTP (local test server). The only approved fake is at the AI-provider (or equivalent third-party-SDK) boundary.\n- Behavior over implementation — assert on observable state, not on call counts or invocation sequences.\n- Deterministic factory functions / fakers over static JSON fixtures.\n- Test names describe behavior (\"creates invoice when all line items valid\"), not positions (\"test_create_invoice_3\").\n- Testing pyramid as a *shape guide* (more unit than integration than e2e), not as mandated ratios.\n- **Performance measurements require a baseline and clear boundaries.** Any claim that something is \"fast\", \"scales\", or \"improved performance\" must include: (a) a numeric baseline to compare against (prior version, prior run, published SLO), (b) an explicit boundary — what unit of work is being measured, what environment, what is excluded (cold-start, warmup, network latency, etc.), (c) reproducible harness so the measurement can be re-run. \"It feels fast\" and \"benchmarks look good\" are not performance claims.\n\n**STRIP (cargo-cult testing dogma — do NOT ship):**\n- Hard coverage percentages as universal rules (\"\u003e= 80% overall\", \"100% on critical paths\"). Replace with: \"measure coverage where the project tracks it; coverage is a signal, not a target\".\n- Specific timing budgets as universal rules (\"unit \u003c 10ms\", \"integration \u003c 100ms\"). Replace with: \"tests should be fast enough that developers run them; the specific threshold is a project concern\".\n- Specific pyramid percentages (\"60% unit / 25% integration / 10% contract / 5% e2e\") — keep the shape, drop the numbers.\n- TDD as the only valid approach. Test-first is one workflow; test-alongside is another; test-after for a specific reason can be defensible. Do not call non-TDD \"worst practice\".\n- Tests-for-the-sake-of-tests (mirror-of-spec tests that add no signal, trivial getters/setters).\n- Mutation testing as a quality requirement. Mention as an optional signal, not a bar to clear.\n- Snapshot tests without a maintenance plan.\n- Retry loops around flaky tests as \"improvement\" — a retry loop hides a bug; fix the flake.\n- Branch coverage vs line coverage dogma — say \"coverage metrics matter less than whether tests catch real bugs\".\n\n**STRIP (DDx-internal specifics):**\n- References to cli/internal/agent/script.go (replace with generic \"a deterministic script-like fake at the AI-provider boundary\")\n- Named DDx legacy offenders (orchTestGitOps, fakeExecuteBeadGit, gateTestGitOps, mockExecutor) — these belong in a helix-test-engineer plugin persona, not this generic base\n- Authoritative-sources frontmatter section citing docs/helix/concerns.md and HELIX concern.md\n- Specific paths like cli/internal/bead, cli/internal/agent\n\nGround in external canon:\n- Kent Beck - Test-Driven Development: By Example\n- Martin Fowler - \"Mocks Arent Stubs\" (canonical reference for why call-sequence assertions are a code smell)\n- Brendan Gregg - Systems Performance methodology (for the baseline + boundary stance on perf measurement)\n- Anthropic Prompt Library (if a testing entry exists)\n- TDD Guard from anthropics/skills repo (if the automated-test-first-enforcement pattern is worth mentioning)\n\nBody structure: Philosophy / Approach / Anti-patterns / Sources.\n\nFrontmatter:\n---\nname: test-engineer\nroles: [test-engineer, quality-analyst]\ndescription: Testing specialist enforcing stubs-over-mocks, contract tests, real e2e, behavior-over-implementation, testing pyramid as shape not ratios, and baselined performance measurement\ntags: [testing, quality, performance]\n---\n\nIn-scope: library/personas/test-engineer.md (new)\nOut-of-scope: deletion of test-engineer-tdd.md (deprecation bead); helix-test-engineer plugin persona (HELIX concern)","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:37:54.294846139Z","id":"ddx-13ff86c6","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:personas"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-94c28517","priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Generalize test-engineer persona — strip DDx-internal path references","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:39:32.555980009Z"}

cli/internal/server/workers.go wires WorkerManager as the waker.\n  - cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go consumes the wake signal via\n    SleepOrWake, dropping the idle-poll sleep when an approve fires.\n  - Tests: TestOperatorPromptApprove_SignalsWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptSubmit_AutoApprove_SignalsWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptCancel_DoesNotSignalWake,\n    TestOperatorPromptApprove_NilWakerNoCrash\n    (cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_operator_prompt_wake_test.go).\n\nAC2 — \"origin_operator_prompt_id added to events / artifact frontmatter\":\n  - cli/internal/agent/operator_prompt_backlinks.go computes the\n    base..result diff after a successful operator-prompt execution,\n    classifies changed paths into affected beads vs artifacts, and\n    appends an `origin_operator_prompt_id` BeadEvent to every affected\n    bead plus a summary event on the originator listing every affected\n    bead and artifact path.\n  - The wiring fires from execute_bead_loop.go ~L794-810 inside the\n    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess branch, gated on\n    candidate.IssueType == bead.IssueTypeOperatorPrompt.\n  - The events form satisfies the spec wording in\n    docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-021 §Bead shape: \"every artifact\n    mutated by an operator-prompt execution gets an\n    origin_operator_prompt_id event so provenance survives later edits\".\n  - Tests: TestComputeOperatorPromptAffected_DiffsBeadsAndArtifacts,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_AppendsEventsOnAffectedAndOriginator,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_EmptyAffectedNoEvents,\n    TestRecordOperatorPromptBacklinks_ConcurrentTargetSameBead.\n\nAC3 — \"Backlinks queryable via ddx bead show\":\n  - `ddx bead show \u003cid\u003e --json` calls Store.LoadEventsInline (cmd/bead.go\n    L330) which inlines events for both active and archived beads, so\n    `origin_operator_prompt_id` events are visible via the JSON shape:\n        ddx bead show \u003coriginator-or-affected-id\u003e --json \\\n          | jq '.events[] | select(.kind == \"origin_operator_prompt_id\")'\n  - Equivalently, `ddx bead evidence list \u003cid\u003e` (cmd/bead.go L654-668)\n    enumerates the same events.\n\nThe verification_command above re-runs the four AC-bearing test packages\nagainst the current tree. .ddx/beads.jsonl:822:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-loop` does not launch open epics or other structurally non-executable bead types by default; the generic execution-ready validation surface rejects or skips such beads before claim/launch; and deterministic tests cover a queue where an open epic sorts ahead of a ready task so the loop selects the task instead of the epic.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-14T03:35:25Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1106936","closing_commit_sha":"8b60eb49955aa807267a5d8aa80fab5e65c8b1b6","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.589044661Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-b44ca394","depends_on_id":"ddx-cf340665","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:30:57Z"}],"description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX queue. Evidence includes the generated execution bundle `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, worktree `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7`, and the current execute-loop implementation in `cli/internal/bead/store.go` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.\n2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.\n3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.\n4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.\n\n## Observed gap\n`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.\n\n## Why this matters\nOpen epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Body is an overview + vocabulary + intent-router directive that grounds Claude/Codex/etc. in the portable DDx model.\n\nFrontmatter (portable minimum — only name + description):\n---\nname: ddx\ndescription: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says \"do work\", \"drain the queue\", \"run the next bead\", \"execute a bead\", \"review this\", \"check against spec\", \"what's on the queue\", \"what's ready\", \"create a bead\", \"file this as work\", \"run an agent\", \"dispatch\", \"use a persona\", \"how am I doing\", \"ddx doctor\", or mentions any ddx CLI command.\n---\n\nNo argument-hint, when_to_use, context: fork, allowed-tools, disable-model-invocation, user-invocable, paths, model, effort, agent, or hooks — those are Claude-Code extensions that break on Codex.\n\nBody structure (under 500 lines):\n1. One-paragraph summary of DDx and what the skill covers.\n2. Vocabulary (inline glossary, single source of truth): bead, queue, ready, blocked, claim, close, work, execute-bead, execute-loop, execution, agent, harness, persona, role, binding, profile, plugin, skill, update vs upgrade, review (bead-review vs quorum review), governing artifact, spec-id. One line per term with the CLI command it maps to.\n3. Intent router — preceded by the explicit directive: 'Before responding to any DDx-related request, read the matching reference file below. The router is not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's guidance, not this overview alone.' Then a table mapping phrases to reference/*.md files.\n4. Top-level policy reminders (one-liners): never edit .ddx/beads.jsonl directly; tracker changes are commit-worthy; plain merge-only for branches with execute-bead commits.\n5. Links out: ddx --help, docs/helix/01-frame/features/.\n\nIn-scope files:\n- skills/ddx/SKILL.md (new)\n\nOut-of-scope (file separately):\n- skills/ddx/reference/*.md — child beads\n- skills/ddx/evals/ — child bead\n- Init changes, embed.go, Makefile — child beads\n\nSource material: existing /skills/ddx-*/SKILL.md files (content to distill). Strip HELIX-specific rules (helix label requirement, phase:* enumeration, TDD-as-mandatory framing).","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-17T22:17:53.914224819Z","id":"ddx-bd852e86","issue_type":"task","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:implementation","area:skills"],"owner":"erik","parent":"ddx-f586b4fd","priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-011","status":"closed","title":"Create skills/ddx/SKILL.md with vocabulary and intent-router directive","updated_at":"2026-04-17T22:19:27.422935829Z"}

Clicking 'Apply fix' calls mutation; on success issue disappears and graph refreshes.\n5. File-changed error: inline error message in issue row; copy-snippet fallback unchanged.\n6. in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.\n\nBackend mutation:\n- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult\n- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP\n- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }\n- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.\n- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.\n\nAuto-repairable strategies:\n- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter\n- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)\n- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map\n\nUI changes to IntegrityPanel:\n- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)\n- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change\n- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)\n- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast\n- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback\n\nFailure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{\"resolved_provider\":\"\",\"fallback_chain\":[]}","created_at":"2026-04-30T19:09:26.294626056Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent .ddx/beads.jsonl:1467:{"acceptance":"Given a plugin package has a valid package.yaml but a declared skill directory missing SKILL.md or required frontmatter, when `ddx install --local` or registry install runs, then the command exits non-zero, writes no installed.yaml entry, and surfaces the same structural validation error that doctor would report","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:33:09Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"234160","closing_commit_sha":"516fc8c8a7d25dbc47e3f8ba694bb2646b2c8d67","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:20:55.587100214Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: cli/cmd/install.go:212\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: high\nDescription: The new install paths only validate `package.yaml`, then proceed to symlink/copy skills without reusing the structural checks that doctor performs. `installLocal` calls `LoadPackageManifest` and then links skills, while `InstallPackage` does the same for tarball installs. As a result, malformed plugins still install successfully. Repro: a local plugin with a valid package.yaml and a declared skill directory containing `bad-skill/` but no `SKILL.md` installs successfully instead of failing fast.\nSuggested fix: Share one structural validator between install and doctor so install rejects missing `SKILL.md`, invalid frontmatter, and related contract violations before writing install state.","id":"hx-f7e65bcc","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:high"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"cli/cmd/install.go","status":"closed","title":"correctness: fail install when plugin skill metadata is invalid","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:33:45.197279785Z"}

.ddx/executions/20260501T041653-b64ff2e8/prompt.md:5:Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.

Backend mutation:
- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult
- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP
- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }
- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.
- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.

Auto-repairable strategies:
- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter
- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)
- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map

UI changes to IntegrityPanel:
- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)
- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change
- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)
- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast
- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback

Failure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).
.ddx/executions/20260501T041653-b64ff2e8/prompt.md:8:1. graphRepairIssue mutation in schema; resolver atomically applies structured edit and re-validates.
2. IntegrityPanel shows 'Preview fix' for REMOVE_MISSING_DEP and APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID issues.
3. Preview expands inline showing frontmatter diff.
4. Clicking 'Apply fix' calls mutation; on success issue disappears and graph refreshes.
5. File-changed error: inline error message in issue row; copy-snippet fallback unchanged.
6. Ambiguous or non-auto-repairable issues: no 'Preview fix' shown; copy-snippet only.
7. Go resolver test covers: successful repair, stale file hash rejection, path traversal rejection.

.ddx/executions/20260430T190925-a1edeb1e/manifest.json:12:    "description": "Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.\n\nBackend mutation:\n- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult\n- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP\n- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }\n- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.\n- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.\n\nAuto-repairable strategies:\n- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter\n- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)\n- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map\n\nUI changes to IntegrityPanel:\n- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)\n- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change\n- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)\n- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast\n- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback\n\nFailure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).",

.ddx/executions/20260430T190925-a1edeb1e/prompt.md:5:Add in-place repair actions to IntegrityPanel for auto-repairable integrity issues.

Backend mutation:
- graphRepairIssue(issueID: ID!, strategy: RepairStrategy!): GraphRepairResult
- RepairStrategy enum: REMOVE_MISSING_DEP, APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID, CLEAN_PATH_MAP
- GraphRepairResult: { success: Boolean!, newIssues: [GraphIssue!]!, error: String }
- Resolver: validates path is within project root, checks current file hash matches issue snapshot, applies structured YAML/frontmatter edit atomically, re-runs graph validation, returns updated issue list.
- NOT auto-repairable server-side: parse errors, cycles, ambiguous duplicates where both files have inbound references, missing required roots.

Auto-repairable strategies:
- REMOVE_MISSING_DEP: removes missing ID from depends_on array in frontmatter
- APPLY_SUGGESTED_ID: renames duplicate ID to the SHA-1 suffix suggestion (only for the one file that has no inbound references)
- CLEAN_PATH_MAP: updates stale path metadata in id_to_path map

UI changes to IntegrityPanel:
- Each auto-repairable issue shows 'Preview fix' button (in addition to existing copy-snippet)
- Preview expands to show a before/after diff of the frontmatter change
- 'Apply fix' button calls the mutation with confirmation (no additional modal for single-file fixes)
- On success: issue removed from panel; graph data refreshes; success toast
- On error (stale file, write failure, new issue introduced): inline error in issue row; no partial success hidden; copy-snippet still available as manual fallback

Failure modes handled: file changed since issue computed, YAML parse failure, permission/path traversal, repair introduces new graph issue, generated file (show warning 'this file may be auto-generated'), concurrent repair (server-side file hash check).

Scope of PRD update:

(1) Restructure Problem section into 6 clusters keyed to thesis principles in product-vision.md:
- Abstraction (no structure / no composition / no integrity guarantees / no transferability)
- Iteration over tracked work (no reusable work-item store / no reusable execution evidence)
- Methodology plurality (no reusable agent dispatch — every workflow tool reinvents)
- LLM physics (NEW cluster — token cost as a permanent constraint; nothing in current PRD)
- Evidence/provenance (no provenance for generated artifacts; no measurement; no feedback capture)
- Human-AI collaboration (no composition for handoffs; no discoverability; no network access)

Existing problem bullets re-grouped under these headers; add the LLM-physics cluster.

(2) Goals (primary): reword #1; add three-layer-architecture goal; add generated-artifact-regeneration goal; add 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP goal.

(3) Non-goals: sharpen loop wording — DDx owns mechanical queue drain (ddx work); content-aware supervisory decisions remain plugin/HELIX. Add: 'DDx does not catalog run types beyond the three layers.'

(4) Feature blurbs: FEAT-001 (CLI: run/try/work top-level + ddx agent passthrough); FEAT-005 (multi-media identity); FEAT-006 (layer-1 consumer + structural passthrough); FEAT-007 (sidecar + generated_by + separate staleness); FEAT-008/021 (media-type rendering + regenerate + layer-aware run views); FEAT-010 (3-layer architecture + substrate unification); FEAT-019 (child of FEAT-010 — evaluation UX, workflow shapes -> skills).

Summary: artifacts (multi-media); three-layer run architecture; one substrate; invocation upstream.

Do NOT touch docs/helix/01-frame/principles.md.

`ddx agent execute-loop` selected and launched an open `epic` bead from the HELIX execution queue instead of a bounded executable bead.

## Reproduction
1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, run `ddx bead ready --json --execution`.
2. Observe the first candidate is `helix-fef22846`, `issue_type=epic`, title `Audit deleted HELIX artifact types for restoration or retirement`.
3. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json`.
4. Observe DDx creates `.ddx/.execute-bead-wt-helix-fef22846-20260411T011535-bbda19d7` and `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json` with `bead_id=helix-fef22846`.

## Observed gap
`ReadyExecution()` currently treats any open, unblocked bead as execution-ready unless `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, or cooldown metadata says otherwise. `execute-loop` then takes the first candidate without a stronger structural execution screen. That behavior is weaker than the API-001 execute-bead supervisor contract, which says the loop should run a generic execution-ready validator against ordered candidates before launch.

## Why this matters
Open epics represent umbrella planning/decomposition work, not necessarily a bounded workspace transform. Launching them directly makes queue-drain behavior unstable and can strand the loop on non-executable work even when bounded tasks are ready behind the epic.

.ddx/executions/20260423T042946-811098c4/manifest.json:13:    "description": "## Observed\n\n`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas` is a well-crafted browser + bind UX, but it is read-only: no add, no edit, no delete, no explanation of what the reader is looking at or why they cannot change it. A user looking at it can reasonably ask \"what is this for?\" and \"why can't I change anything?\" and the UI answers neither.\n\n## Ground truth\n\n- **What a persona is.** A markdown file with frontmatter under `library/personas/*.md` defining AI personality, expertise, principles, communication style. Shipped defaults: architect, code-reviewer, implementer, specification-enforcer, test-engineer.\n- **How it's used.** Projects declare role→persona bindings in `.ddx/config.yaml` via `persona_bindings`. When an agent performs role X, the bound persona's body is injected into its system prompt.\n- **What the tool exposes today.**\n  - CLI: `ddx persona --list`, `--show \u003cname\u003e`, `--bind \u003cpersona\u003e --role \u003crole\u003e`. No create/edit/delete.\n  - GraphQL: `personaBind` mutation only (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:150`). No `personaCreate/Update/Delete` in schema.\n  - UI: list/show/bind parity with CLI.\n- **Why it evolved this way.** Personas have been treated as *library content* — authored carefully, version-controlled upstream, pulled into projects via `ddx update` and contributed back via `ddx contribute`. The tool deliberately did not become a persona editor.\n\n## The gap\n\nThe library-only model doesn't hold up for real use:\n\n1. **Project-local personas are a real need.** Teams want \"our code-reviewer, with our opinions about our codebase.\" Today they either (a) fork the persona file and push upstream (wrong — it's not generally useful), (b) never customize (bad outcomes), or (c) drop a file into some ad-hoc local path and hope it works.\n2. **The UI implies capability it doesn't have.** A list + detail view with no hint about source policy reads as \"unfinished feature\" rather than \"intentional read-only\".\n3. **Binding alone isn't enough.** Even if editing stays out of scope, users need to understand *which personas are available*, *where they come from* (library vs project-local), and *how to change that*.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nTwo decisions (pick before implementation):\n\n### D1 — Policy: who owns persona content?\n\nRecommend: **both, with a clear boundary.**\n- Library personas: `library/personas/*.md` — read-only in UI, contribute upstream via `ddx contribute`.\n- Project-local personas: new directory `.ddx/personas/*.md` — fully editable in UI, live with the project, not in the shared library. Override semantics: a project-local persona with the same name as a library persona takes precedence for that project's bindings.\n\n### D2 — Scope of the UI\n\nMinimum viable, given D1:\n- **Explainer copy** on the personas page header: \"Personas are AI personality templates. Library personas are shared; project personas live with this project. Bind a persona to a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`.\"\n- **Source badge** on each persona row: `library` or `project`.\n- **Project-local editor.** For project personas:\n  - `New persona` button → form (name, roles/tags, body markdown). Creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` with frontmatter.\n  - `Edit` on project rows.\n  - `Delete` on project rows with confirm.\n- **Library rows stay read-only.** Action on a library row: `Fork to project` — copies the library file to `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` (with a default rename suggestion on collision) and opens the editor. No in-place edit, no delete.\n- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.\n\n## What this bead does NOT decide\n\n- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.\n- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.\n- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.\n- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).\n\n## CLI parity\n\nWhatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too. Add:\n- `ddx persona new \u003cname\u003e` — creates `.ddx/personas/\u003cname\u003e.md` scaffold.\n- `ddx persona edit \u003cname\u003e` — opens `$EDITOR` on the project-local persona (errors on library).\n- `ddx persona fork \u003clibrary-name\u003e [--as \u003cnew-name\u003e]` — copies library → project.\n- `ddx persona delete \u003cname\u003e` — project-local only.\n\nUI and CLI call the same underlying `cli/internal/persona` operations; no duplicate logic.",
.ddx/executions/20260423T042946-811098c4/prompt.md:5:## Observed

`/nodes/.../projects/.../personas` is a well-crafted browser + bind UX, but it is read-only: no add, no edit, no delete, no explanation of what the reader is looking at or why they cannot change it. A user looking at it can reasonably ask "what is this for?" and "why can't I change anything?" and the UI answers neither.

## Ground truth

- **What a persona is.** A markdown file with frontmatter under `library/personas/*.md` defining AI personality, expertise, principles, communication style. Shipped defaults: architect, code-reviewer, implementer, specification-enforcer, test-engineer.
- **How it's used.** Projects declare role→persona bindings in `.ddx/config.yaml` via `persona_bindings`. When an agent performs role X, the bound persona's body is injected into its system prompt.
- **What the tool exposes today.**
  - CLI: `ddx persona --list`, `--show <name>`, `--bind <persona> --role <role>`. No create/edit/delete.
  - GraphQL: `personaBind` mutation only (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:150`). No `personaCreate/Update/Delete` in schema.
  - UI: list/show/bind parity with CLI.
- **Why it evolved this way.** Personas have been treated as *library content* — authored carefully, version-controlled upstream, pulled into projects via `ddx update` and contributed back via `ddx contribute`. The tool deliberately did not become a persona editor.

## The gap

The library-only model doesn't hold up for real use:

1. **Project-local personas are a real need.** Teams want "our code-reviewer, with our opinions about our codebase." Today they either (a) fork the persona file and push upstream (wrong — it's not generally useful), (b) never customize (bad outcomes), or (c) drop a file into some ad-hoc local path and hope it works.
2. **The UI implies capability it doesn't have.** A list + detail view with no hint about source policy reads as "unfinished feature" rather than "intentional read-only".
3. **Binding alone isn't enough.** Even if editing stays out of scope, users need to understand *which personas are available*, *where they come from* (library vs project-local), and *how to change that*.

## Proposed direction

Two decisions (pick before implementation):

### D1 — Policy: who owns persona content?

Recommend: **both, with a clear boundary.**
- Library personas: `library/personas/*.md` — read-only in UI, contribute upstream via `ddx contribute`.
- Project-local personas: new directory `.ddx/personas/*.md` — fully editable in UI, live with the project, not in the shared library. Override semantics: a project-local persona with the same name as a library persona takes precedence for that project's bindings.

### D2 — Scope of the UI

Minimum viable, given D1:
- **Explainer copy** on the personas page header: "Personas are AI personality templates. Library personas are shared; project personas live with this project. Bind a persona to a role in `.ddx/config.yaml`."
- **Source badge** on each persona row: `library` or `project`.
- **Project-local editor.** For project personas:
  - `New persona` button → form (name, roles/tags, body markdown). Creates `.ddx/personas/<name>.md` with frontmatter.
  - `Edit` on project rows.
  - `Delete` on project rows with confirm.
- **Library rows stay read-only.** Action on a library row: `Fork to project` — copies the library file to `.ddx/personas/<name>.md` (with a default rename suggestion on collision) and opens the editor. No in-place edit, no delete.
- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.

## What this bead does NOT decide

- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.
- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.
- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.
- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).

## CLI parity

Whatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too. Add:
- `ddx persona new <name>` — creates `.ddx/personas/<name>.md` scaffold.
- `ddx persona edit <name>` — opens `$EDITOR` on the project-local persona (errors on library).
- `ddx persona fork <library-name> [--as <new-name>]` — copies library → project.
- `ddx persona delete <name>` — project-local only.

UI and CLI call the same underlying `cli/internal/persona` operations; no duplicate logic.

No in-place edit, no delete.\n- **Bind affordance remains.** Binding UI continues to work for both sources.\n\n## What this bead does NOT decide\n\n- Multi-project persona sharing (e.g., org-level personas between library and project). File a follow-up if it becomes pressing.\n- Rich editor affordances beyond a plain markdown textarea with preview. Frontmatter can be a YAML-ish textarea for v1.\n- Syncing project personas upstream — that remains `ddx contribute` and is out of scope.\n- Any change to how personas are injected into agent prompts (the read path on the server).\n\n## CLI parity\n\nWhatever the UI gains, the CLI gains too.
